era_context: Identical to -2290, -2291, and -2292: Old Kingdom Egypt in decline, Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin, Indus Valley Civilization in mature phase, Early Bronze Age III in the Levant and Anatolia.

events.title: Akkadian Empire: military and administrative consolidation
events.description: Naram-Sin continues military campaigns and administrative consolidation. His reign represents the apex of Akkadian territorial expansion, though the empire faces mounting pressure from Gutian tribes in the Zagros and from Amorite incursions in the west. The empire's military and administrative apparatus is stretched to maintain control over conquered territories.
events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's reign is well-attested. The specific dating to -2293 depends on chronological systems.

events.title: Indus Valley Civilization: mature phase stability
events.description: The Indus Valley Civilization continues its stable, hierarchical urban civilization. No major changes are evident in the archaeological record.

sources.name: Archaeological stratigraphy at Indus Valley sites

events.certainty_note: The mature phase is archaeologically well-attested; year-by-year dating is not possible.

events.title: Egyptian Sixth Dynasty: provincial autonomy increases
events.description: The Sixth Dynasty continues its gradual loss of central authority. Provincial governors accumulate power and resources.
events.certainty_note: The trend is well-attested; year-by-year dating is conventional.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record for -2293 is indistinguishable from -2290, -2291, and -2292. The assignment of distinct events to each year is artificial and reflects the limitations of ancient chronology. For this period, year-by-year precision is not achievable with available sources.

graph_edges.note: Part of the same broader pattern of Akkadian military expansion
